title: Advanced Encoding & Codecs Mastery
description: Master modern video codecs from theory to practical implementation, focusing on next-generation VVC/H.266 and codec optimization techniques.

resources.title: uvg266 - Open-source VVC/H.266 Encoder
resources.description: Open-source VVC/H.266 encoder licensed under 3-clause BSD, developed by University of Helsinki's Ultra Video Group. Still under active development with ongoing RD-quality and speed improvements.

resources.title: x266 - Next Generation VVC/H.266 Encoder by MulticoreWare
resources.description: Open-source VVC/H.266 compliant encoder built on top of x265, featuring HDR/WCG support, 360° video encoding, and SDR video. Developed for achieving twice the coding efficiency of HEVC.

resources.title: VVCEasy
resources.description: Ready-to-use prebuilt VVC (H.266) binaries and players for Windows, macOS, Linux, FreeBSD, Haiku, Android, and WebAssembly. Includes vvdec, vvenc, uvg266 encoders/decoders, and FFmpeg builds with VVC support.

resources.title: JVET VVC Test Model (VTM) Reference Software
resources.description: Official overview site for Versatile Video Coding (VVC) and the VVC Test Model (VTM) reference software from the Joint Video Experts Team. Hosts VTM encoder/decoder, experimental JEM software, and Benchmark Set (BMS) tools.

resources.title: libmpeg2
resources.description: A free library for decoding MPEG-1 and MPEG-2 video streams. It's lightweight and portable, used in several open source media players for MPEG-2 playback.

resources.title: slowmoVideo
resources.description: An open source tool that creates slow-motion videos from existing footage by interpolating intermediate frames, allowing extreme slow motion effects. Note: the official site is served over plain HTTP — the host does not support HTTPS.

resources.title: QWinFF
resources.description: A simple cross-platform FFmpeg GUI (using Qt) for converting audio and video, providing preset profiles and the ability to batch process files.

milestones.title: Codec Fundamentals & VVC Introduction
milestones.description: Understand VVC/H.266 architecture, compression principles, and compare with legacy codecs like H.264 and HEVC.

milestones.title: Hands-On Encoding with Open-Source Tools
milestones.description: Implement encoding workflows using uvg266 and x266 encoders, experiment with encoding parameters and quality settings.

milestones.title: Performance Analysis & Benchmarking
milestones.description: Benchmark codec performance, analyze efficiency metrics, and optimize encoding for different use cases.

milestones.title: Integration & Automation
milestones.description: Build automated encoding pipelines using FFmpeg tools and Python bindings for production workflows.

prerequisites: Basic understanding of video compression concepts
Knowledge of H.264 or HEVC fundamentals
learningObjectives: Master VVC/H.266 codec architecture and compression techniques
Implement efficient encoding workflows with multiple codec options
Benchmark and optimize codec performance for specific scenarios
Automate video processing pipelines with FFmpeg and Python
Evaluate trade-offs between quality, speed, and file size

title: Advanced Protocols & Transport for Video Streaming
description: Master modern video streaming protocols and transport mechanisms. From protocol analysis to adaptive bitrate delivery and peer-to-peer technologies for real-time communication.

resources.title: multicat
resources.description: A set of lightweight tools for multicast and transport stream manipulation (from the VLC project) – used for efficient UDP streaming, recording, and conversion of TS streams.

resources.title: PeerJS
resources.description: A simple peer-to-peer JavaScript library that abstracts WebRTC for direct video, audio, and data connectivity between browsers. Provides an easy way to build video chat and P2P apps.

resources.title: coturn
resources.description: An open source implementation of TURN and STUN servers, used to facilitate NAT traversal for peer-to-peer video (and audio) communication in WebRTC and other protocols.

resources.title: aiortc (Python WebRTC)
resources.description: A Python library for Web Real-Time Communication that implements WebRTC and ORTC, enabling real-time audio/video and data streaming in Python applications.

resources.title: Pion WebRTC
resources.description: A pure Go implementation of the WebRTC protocol, enabling developers to build real-time video (and audio) streaming applications without external dependencies.

resources.title: DVBlast
resources.description: A simple and powerful MPEG-2/TS demux and streaming application. DVBlast can stream digital TV (DVB) channels over a network, often used for IPTV or SAT-IP solutions.

milestones.title: Protocol Fundamentals & Network Analysis
milestones.description: Understand streaming protocols at packet level. Learn RTMP, RTSP, HLS, and DASH foundations using protocol analyzers.

milestones.title: HTTP-Based Adaptive Streaming
milestones.description: Deep dive into DASH and HLS standards. Learn manifest formats, segmentation strategies, and conformance validation.

milestones.title: Real-Time Transport & Peer-to-Peer
milestones.description: Explore WebRTC, NAT traversal, and P2P video transport. Build foundations for live conferencing and peer-assisted delivery.

milestones.title: Low-Latency Streaming Optimization
milestones.description: Master techniques for sub-second latency. Integrate CMAF, optimize transport chains, and apply AI-driven bitrate selection.

milestones.title: Integration & Advanced Transport Manipulation
milestones.description: Combine knowledge through practical projects. Use MPEG-TS tools and implement hybrid delivery systems.

prerequisites: Basic knowledge of video codecs and containers
Familiarity with HTTP and basic web technologies
learningObjectives: Master DASH and HLS protocol specifications and implementations
Analyze and debug streaming video at packet level
Design and implement WebRTC-based real-time video systems
Apply adaptive bitrate algorithms with machine learning insights
Configure and troubleshoot NAT traversal infrastructure
Manipulate and validate MPEG transport streams

title: Cross-Platform Video Player Development
description: Master building video players across desktop, mobile, and web platforms using modern frameworks and libraries.

resources.title: IINA
resources.description: A modern, open source macOS media player built on mpv. IINA features a sleek UI, gesture support, and wide format compatibility.

resources.title: DKVideoPlayer
resources.description: An Android video player library wrapping MediaPlayer, ExoPlayer, and IJKPlayer with features like preload, list playback, floating window, and more.

resources.title: PryntTrimmerView
resources.description: An iOS UI component that provides a video trimming interface, allowing users to select and cut segments of a video with a visual timeline.

resources.title: Swift-YouTube-Player
resources.description: A Swift library for embedding and controlling YouTube videos in iOS applications via a WKWebView.

resources.title: Able Player
resources.description: A fully accessible, cross-browser HTML5 media player library that supports captions, audio descriptions, and sign language tracks, designed to be inclusive for users with disabilities.

resources.title: VideoPlayView
resources.description: A custom Android view that integrates a video player with a loader and placeholder image, enabling quick embedding of video playback in Android apps.

milestones.title: Desktop Player Fundamentals
milestones.description: Learn desktop video playback architecture using mpv and SMPlayer as reference implementations.

milestones.title: Android Video Integration
milestones.description: Build custom Android video players with GPU acceleration and advanced playback controls.

milestones.title: iOS & macOS Video Solutions
milestones.description: Implement video players for Apple platforms using AVPlayer-based frameworks and native UI components.

milestones.title: Web-Based Video Players
milestones.description: Create accessible, cross-browser HTML5 video players with advanced features and responsive design.

milestones.title: Advanced Features & Optimization
milestones.description: Implement hardware acceleration, streaming optimization, and platform-specific enhancements.

prerequisites: Understanding of video codecs (H.264, H.265, VP9)
Basic knowledge of mobile and desktop development
learningObjectives: Build feature-rich video players across multiple platforms
Create accessible and user-friendly playback interfaces
Optimize video streaming and playback performance
Integrate advanced controls like trimming, filtering, and gesture support
